sections.paragraph:
θηλάζω fut. θηλάσω LXX; 1 aor. ἐθήλασα

① to breast-feed an infant, nurse (Lysias, Aristot. et al.; pap, LXX; En 99:5) abs. Mt 24:19; Mk 13:17; Lk 21:23; 23:29 v.l. Ἄννα θηλάζει GJs 6:3 (once in pap, twice in codd.)

② to receive nourishment at the breast, suck, nurse (Hippocr., Aristot.+; PRyl 153, 18; LXX; TestSol 1:2 [cod B] and 4; TestAbr A 6 p. 83, 13 [Stone p. 14]; TestBenj 1:3; ApcEsdr 5:2 p. 29, 25 Tdf.; Philo, Det. Pot. Ins. 118) μαστοὶ οὓς ἐθήλασας the breasts you have sucked Lk 11:27 (cp. SSol 8:1; Job 3:12; Hippocr. VIII 594 L. θ. τὸν μαστόν; Theocr. 3, 16 μασδὸν ἐθήλαξεν). οἱ θηλάζοντες or τὰ θηλάζοντα if the pl. of βρέφος is implied in the gen. pl. θηλαζόντων nursing babies Mt 21:16 (Ps 8:3).—B. 334.—DELG s.v. θηλή. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

aor. aor. = aorist

Lysias Lysias , V–IV b.c.—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

En En = I Enoch—List 2

abs. abs. = absolute

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

GJs GJs = Gospel of James (Protevangelium Jacobi), II a.d.—List 1

Hippocr Hippocr = Corpus Hippocraticum, V–IV b.c.—List 5

PRyl PRyl = Catalogue of the Greek Papyri in the John Rylands Library—List 4

TestSol TestSol = Testament of Solomon, I–III a.d.—List 2

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

TestAbr TestAbr = Testament of Abraham, with some interpolations; I b.c./I a.d.—Lists 2, 5

TestBenj TestBenj = Testament of Benjamin, s. Test12Patr—List 2

ApcEsdr ApcEsdr = Apocalypse of Esdras—List 2

Tdf. Tdf. = CvTischendorf—List 1, beg.

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts
  2. L. = KLachmann, editor of the Greek NT (1842–50)—List 1, beg.

Theocr Theocr , IV–III b.c.—List 5

  1. pl. = plural

gen. gen. = genitive

  1. B. = CDBuck, A Dictionary of Selected Synonyms—List 6

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 455
